student_message: Yeah yeah I get it. Manu is wrong. Next!
final_message:
I can see you might be feeling a little frustrated, and it is true that math can be hard sometimes! Take a big deep breath. You just nailed that line of symmetry riddle perfectly! Remember, making mistakes and taking your time is just a normal part of learning, and I believe in you! 🌟💙 

Since you are doing so well, let's look at a new letter. Think about the big uppercase letter $$H$$. If you wanted to fold it so the halves match perfectly like a mirror, how many lines of symmetry do you think the letter $$H$$ has?

orchestrator_intent.goal: Encourage the student and present a quiz question to test his understanding of lines of symmetry.
orchestrator_intent.reasoning: The student is frustrated and impatient to move on. Encouragement addresses his emotion, while a quiz satisfies his request to advance and tests his actual understanding.

current_section_title: Reflection Symmetry and Lines of Symmetry
current_section_content: A line of symmetry is a fold line that divides a shape into two halves that are perfect mirror images of each other. Some shapes have a vertical line of symmetry (left-right mirror), some have a horizontal line (top-bottom mirror), and some have both. You can use this idea to cut out shapes by drawing only half or one-quarter of the shape on folded paper, then cutting and unfolding. Letters like A have one vertical line of symmetry, while letters like H have both vertical and horizontal lines of symmetry.
